coverage.relationship: Extends the earlier Dresden–Riesa route westwards to Leipzig and adds the Dresden–Coswig line and Friedrichstadt yard.

trainProtection.scope: Supported by the route's included German rolling stock; exact infrastructure coverage still requires observation.
Western high-speed section used by ICE services; precise start and end points require observation.
Available on the included DB BR 411 ICE-T, but Dovetail describes it as less relevant here than on Frankfurt–Fulda.

associated.scope: Can appear when TSW 6 Random Events are enabled.

playerRelevantNotes: Players should be prepared to transition between Hl and Ks visual logic in the Dresden area.
LZB-equipped services may supervise speed on the western part of the route.
Random Events can introduce temporary speed restrictions and signal delays, so atypical restrictive aspects are player-relevant rather than purely decorative.
Generation 8 consoles have a reduced timetable, but the route infrastructure and core BR 442 driving remain in scope.
openQuestions: Map the exact geographical boundary between Hl and Ks signalling.
Identify every displayed Hl and Ks aspect observable in timetable and scenario play.
Map LZB start and end boards and the exact supervised section.
Confirm which subsidiary indicators, speed indicators, shunting signals and boards are represented.
Determine whether Random Event temporary restrictions use the complete real-world Lf board sequence or a simplified subset.

routes.relationship: The route is bundled as an entitlement with Leipzig–Dresden, and the newer route expands its geography.
openQuestions: Confirm whether the original route version has the same Hl/Ks boundary and aspect implementation as the TSW 6 expansion.
Check for signalling fixes or remaster changes between versions.
Catalogue the Riesa, Dresden Hbf, Meißen branch and avoiding-line signal inventories separately.
Confirm the represented lineside systems and transition points.
Confirm PZB, Sifa and GNT operation for the DB BR 612.
Catalogue speed boards and tilting-train-specific infrastructure relevant to players.
Confirm the lineside system or systems across the Leipzig S-Bahn route.
Confirm PZB and Sifa implementation on the DB BR 1442.
Identify any tunnel, compact or S-Bahn-specific signal forms.

dtg-new-horizons-leipzig.title: Train Sim World 6 – New Horizons to Leipzig
